summary: Comprehensive E2E frontend testing of the migrated PHP/MySQL/Bootstrap UCODE SOFTTECH store via the preview URL. The React app has been fully replaced by the PHP site. All 13 critical flows pass: homepage, shop/category, antivirus gosoftwarebuy images, product variant switching, AJAX add-to-cart, cart + ProAssist upsell, checkout -> Stripe hosted page redirect with valid Sandbox session ($209.99 order), AI chat (real reply via Emergent LLM proxy), user register/login/logout, admin login + dashboard (live stats $79.99 revenue, 1 paid order, 37 products, best-sellers list), all admin tabs (Dashboard/Products/Orders/Leads/Key Inventory/Emails), product price editor (37 rows with editable price/was inputs + per-row Save), static pages (about-us, sitemap, disclaimer, faqs, blog), dark-mode toggle with localStorage persistence, currency (USD) selector.

issues: Revenue chart is rendered as CSS div-bars (not <canvas>/<svg>). Functional and visually correct (30 daily bars with tooltips), but if a real charting library was intended, swap to Chart.js/ApexCharts for richer interactivity. Not a bug.

Card number/exp/cvv inputs on checkout.php are intentionally decorative (no name attribute) because real entry happens on the Stripe hosted page after submit — this matches the documented design. Consider adding a subtle 'Powered by Stripe — card details collected on next step' helper text to avoid user confusion.

critical_code_review_comments: All key interactive elements expose data-testid (version-option-*, edition-option-*, os-option-*, pd-add-to-cart, admin-tab-*, dashboard-revenue-chart, dashboard-best-sellers, etc.) — excellent for testability.

Stripe integration verified end-to-end through the Emergent proxy: submitting the PHP checkout 302-redirects to checkout.stripe.com/c/pay/cs_test_... with the cart total preserved (Sandbox banner shown).
AI chat (ajax/chat.php) returns coherent, on-topic replies via Emergent LLM proxy in <10s.
Admin auth is enforced (admin.php -> login.php when logged out; account.php -> login.php?next=account.php after logout).
Bitdefender category uses real gosoftwarebuy.com box-shot images as required.
Product variant navigation works: clicking version-option-2021 from the 2024 Pro Plus page navigates to /product.php?slug=microsoft-office-2021-home-business-windows.
Dark-mode preference persists across full page reload via localStorage/cookie.
Stat cards on /admin.php?tab=dashboard reflect real DB state: 37 Products, 5 Orders, 1 Paid, $79.99 Total Revenue, 1 Lead, 0 Keys Avail, 1 Email Queued.
test_credentials: Admin admin@ucodesofttechus.com / Admin@UC2026! verified. Buyer test account auto-created during test: TEST_buyer_<ts>@example.com / Test@1234 -> redirects to /account.php on success.
seed_data_creation: Created one buyer account (TEST_buyer_*@example.com) and one open Stripe Sandbox checkout session (no payment completed). Cart contained Microsoft Office 2024 Professional Plus (Windows) at $209.99 during checkout test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: PHP site is the active app (React fully replaced). Use REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL from /app/frontend/.env. Page URLs are .php (e.g. /product.php?slug=...). Key data-testids: brand-logo, nav-microsoft, cart-count, pd-add-to-cart, version-option-{2024|2021|2019}, edition-option-{home-and-business|professional-plus|home-and-student|home}, os-option-{pc|mac}, login-submit, register-submit, admin-page, admin-tab-{dashboard|products|orders|leads|keys|emails}, dashboard-revenue-chart, dashboard-best-sellers, chat-input, chat-send, lead-name/lead-email/lead-phone. The login Sign In button is a normal button[type=submit] inside the login form — when locating, prefer 'form button[type=submit]' or '[data-testid=login-submit]' because the chat widget also contains a button[type=submit] (data-testid=chat-send) earlier in DOM order. Admin price inputs are type=text (not type=number) — locate via 'table tbody tr input.form-control' or row-scoped selectors.
rca of the issue: No production bugs found. Only minor self-test selector issues in my own script (e.g., using button[type=submit].first matched the chat widget's send button on login.php). Fixed in subsequent runs by scoping to form button[type=submit] or data-testid=login-submit. No code changes required.
